description: This figure presents two parametric plots illustrating the isothermal oxidation behavior of Fe-Mn-Al alloys at 800°C after 24 hours in pure oxygen (200 torr). Subplot (a) displays weight gain per unit area (ΔW/24, mg cm⁻²) as a function of manganese concentration for alloys with fixed aluminum contents of 6, 7, 8, and 9 wt% (labeled 6AL, 7AL, 8AL, 9AL). Subplot (b) shows weight gain versus aluminum concentration for alloys containing fixed manganese levels of 2.5, 5, 10, 15, and 20 wt% Mn (denoted by solid circles, crosses, triangles, squares, and open circles respectively). Both plots reveal characteristic minima in oxidation rates: subplot (a) shows reduced weight gains for alloys containing 5–10% Mn, while subplot (b) exhibits minima at approximately 6–9% Al. These minima correspond to alloys forming continuous protective alumina scales (Region III), with optimal oxidation resistance occurring in the composition range Fe–(5–10)% Mn–(6–10)% Al. The curves demonstrate that low manganese additions (<2%) increase oxidation rates by promoting iron-rich oxide nodules, whereas manganese levels above 7.5% lead to manganese-rich scales. In subplot (b), weight gains increase again at aluminum concentrations exceeding ~9% due to the formation of small manganese oxide nodules penetrating the alumina scale.
